textPlain: Been thinking about this lately and ultimately, I'm thinking that -- taking into account what we know about "federation" --  both the Nostr and ATProto models are generally pointless because they attack a problem with more complicated tech that must be solved with OR without that tech anyway.Someone said it really well; if your solution relies on "maybe people will learn about or do new complex thing X" it's just not likely to take off.But for the sake of argument, let's try going down that road for this. Along the way you'll be communicating with people, building trust, etc etc.But now YOU'VE ALREADY DONE THE THING YOU'RE trying to optimize for, and for which we already have an extremely resilient model, aka Mastodon-which-is-very-analogous-to-email.  At that point, just make a mastodon server or servers with with those people.It just feels like the smart bet is doing that analogously to email, a model that definitely works, then trying to do the same thing PLUS invent a whole new idea of "take everything with you" at the user level.
nostr started as very simple but soon there are like millions of NIPs.
Bummer that all three bluesky links in the intro are dead links now, and the author's bluesky account appears to be deactivated:https://bsky.app/profile/shreyanjain.net
By contrast, NOSTR comments continue to work just fine.Quite telling between centralized vs decentralized environments. NOSTR is indeed more resilient.
